key.name: always-visible
key.summary: Indicator is always visible
key.description: If true, the indicator is always visible even when no updates are pending.

key.name: show-count
key.summary: Show update count on indicator
key.description: Display the number of pending updates next to the icon.

key.name: show-timechecked
key.summary: Show last checked time in menu
key.description: Display the date and time of the last update check in the menu.

key.name: notify
key.summary: Send notification when new updates are available
key.description: Show a GNOME notification when new updates are detected.

key.name: boot-wait
key.summary: Seconds to wait before first check at startup
key.description: Delay (in seconds) before the first automatic update check after login.

key.name: check-interval
key.summary: Interval between update checks (minutes)
key.description: How often to check for updates automatically, in minutes. Set to 0 to disable.

key.name: check-cmd
key.summary: Command used to check for available updates
key.description: Shell command to list available updates. Must output one update per line in zypper format.

key.name: update-cmd-options
key.summary: How to apply updates
key.description: 0=GNOME Software, 1=GNOME PackageKit, 2=Terminal: zypper dup, 3=Terminal: zypper ref+dup, 4=Terminal: zypper dup + flatpak update, 5=Terminal: zypper ref+dup + flatpak update, 6=Terminal: flatpak update only, 7=Custom command

key.name: update-cmd
key.summary: Custom update command
key.description: Command to run when "Custom" is selected as the update method.

key.name: terminal
key.summary: Terminal emulator open command
key.description: Command to open the terminal window. The extension always appends "bash -c SCRIPT" after this. Examples: "gnome-terminal --", "tilix -e", "xterm -e", "konsole -e", "alacritty -e".

key.name: pause-before-close
key.summary: Wait for Enter before closing the terminal
key.description: If true, the terminal window stays open after the update finishes and waits for the user to press Enter.

key.name: check-flatpak
key.summary: Also check for Flatpak updates
key.description: Run a separate flatpak check and include Flatpak apps in the update count and list.

key.name: check-flatpak-cmd
key.summary: Command used to list available Flatpak updates
key.description: Must output tab-separated lines: appId[TAB]newVersion, or appId[TAB]curVersion[TAB]newVersion. Runs via bash -c so pipes and redirections work.

key.name: strip-versions
key.summary: Show only package names in the update list
key.description: If true, version numbers are hidden in the pending update list.

key.name: auto-expand-list
key.summary: Auto-expand update list when count is at most this value
key.description: Automatically expand the update list submenu if the number of updates is less than or equal to this value. 0 = never auto-expand.

key.name: zypper-dir
key.summary: Directory to monitor for package database changes
key.description: When this directory changes (e.g. after applying updates), a new check is scheduled automatically.

key.name: enable-positioning
key.summary: Enable manual panel position
key.description: If true, the indicator will be placed at a specific position in the panel.

key.name: position
key.summary: Panel box (0=left, 1=center, 2=right)

key.name: position-number
key.summary: Index within the chosen panel box

key.name: priv-escalation
key.summary: Privilege escalation method for zypper and system Flatpak updates
key.description: Command used to gain root for zypper and system Flatpak. "sudo" asks in the terminal; "pkexec" shows a graphical dialog; "run0" uses systemd/polkit (graphical on most setups).

key.name: flatpak-user-only
key.summary: Update only user-installed Flatpaks (no root needed)
key.description: If true, passes --user to flatpak update, which never requires elevated privileges. Disable to also update system Flatpaks (requires the configured privilege escalation method).
